DSAI Lab Project

**Milestone 2: Dataset Identification & Preparation**

**Transformer-Based Fake Job Posting Detection**

Section 4.1 - AI-Powered Fraud Classification System

March 2026

# 1\. Data Sources

This project draws on two publicly available datasets and a conditionally applied synthetic augmentation strategy. All sources were verified for accessibility, licensing compliance, and suitability for the fraud classification task prior to use.

## 1.1 Primary Dataset - Kaggle Fake Job Postings

| **Attribute** | **Detail** |
| --- | --- |
| **Source** | Kaggle - shivamb/real-or-fake-fake-jobposting-prediction |
| **Format** | Single CSV file, UTF-8 encoded |
| **Size** | 17,880 job postings; 18 columns (17 features + 1 label) |
| **Label Distribution** | 866 fraudulent (4.84%) \| 17,014 legitimate (95.16%) |
| **License** | CC0 1.0 Public Domain - unrestricted use for research and commercial purposes |
| **Ethical Sourcing** | No PII beyond publicly posted employer details. No special data handling obligations. |
| **Usage Constraint** | None. Freely downloadable via Kaggle API; widely used in published fraud detection research. |

## 1.2 Supplementary Dataset - arshkon LinkedIn Job Postings

| **Attribute** | **Detail** |
| --- | --- |
| **Source** | Kaggle - arshkon/linkedin-job-postings |
| **Format** | CSV (multiple related files) |
| **Size** | 123,000+ job postings |
| **Labels** | No fraud labels - all postings treated as legitimate (negative class only) |
| **License** | CC BY 4.0 - attribution required; permitted for research |
| **Intended Use** | Out-of-distribution (OOD) validation: generalization test to unseen job board distribution |

## 1.3 Synthetic Augmentation - GPT-4 Generated Postings (Conditional)

Synthetic fraud postings are a conditional fallback only - not a default step. They are introduced if fraud recall remains below 0.90 after Focal Loss training with weighted class assignment.

| **Attribute** | **Detail** |
| --- | --- |
| **Generator** | OpenAI GPT-4 API with controlled fraud signal prompts |
| **Fraud Signals Injected** | Vague company details, unrealistic salary, urgency language, PII requests, suspicious domains |
| **Volume Cap** | ≤ 20-30% of total training volume |
| **Trigger Condition** | Activated only if fraud recall < 0.90 after weighted loss training |
| **License** | OpenAI API outputs - permitted for research use under OpenAI terms of service |

# 2\. Dataset Description

The primary dataset provides the backbone for model training, validation, and testing. A full characterisation is provided below.

## 2.1 Dataset Dimensions

| **Property** | **Value** |
| --- | --- |
| **Total Samples** | 17,880 job postings |
| **Fraudulent** | 866 (4.84%) |
| **Legitimate** | 17,014 (95.16%) |
| **Imbalance Ratio** | ~20:1 (legitimate : fraudulent) |
| **Feature Columns** | 17 (5 free-text + 9 structured + 3 binary/categorical) |
| **Label Column** | 1 (fraudulent: 0 = legitimate, 1 = fraud) |
| **Total Columns** | 18 (17 features + 1 label) |
| **File Size** | ~6 MB, single CSV, UTF-8 |

![](data:image/png;base64,)_Figure 2.1 - Class Distribution Bar Chart_

## 2.2 Feature Schema

The 17 feature columns are organised into three groups. The label column (fraudulent) is excluded from this schema.

**Group A - Free-Text Fields (5 columns)**

| **Column** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **title** | Job title as posted - key fraud signal (e.g. 'Work from Home Agent Needed') |
| **description** | Full job description - primary text signal. ~3.5% missing. |
| **requirements** | Required qualifications and skills. ~42% missing. |
| **company_profile** | Hiring company description. ~50% missing - absence itself is a fraud signal. |
| **benefits** | Offered benefits. ~71% missing. |

**Group B - Structured Metadata Fields (9 columns)**

| **Column** | **Missing %** | **Notes** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **location** | <1% | Semi-structured string (City, State, Country) - inconsistent formatting |
| **department** | ~65% | Organisational department name |
| **salary_range** | ~84% | String salary band (e.g. '50000-70000') - inconsistent formats |
| **employment_type** | ~33% | Full-time, Part-time, Contract, Other |
| **required_experience** | ~33% | Experience level (Entry level, Mid-Senior, etc.) |
| **required_education** | ~33% | Minimum education requirement |
| **industry** | ~33% | Industry classification |
| **function** | ~35% | Job function category |

**Group C - Binary / Categorical Fields (3 columns)**

| **Column** | **Values** | **Notes** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **has_company_logo** | 0 / 1 | Strong fraud signal - fraudulent postings disproportionately lack a logo |
| **telecommuting** | 0 / 1 | Whether remote work is offered. Deliberately excluded from STRUCT_COLS - remote status has no meaningful relationship to fraud likelihood and would inject uninformative tokens into the model's attention window. |
| **has_questions** | 0 / 1 | Whether screening questions are included. Deliberately excluded from STRUCT_COLS - presence or absence of screening questions carries no discriminative signal for the fraud/legitimate classification task. |

## 2.3 Token Length Distribution

Each posting is consolidated into a single input sequence by joining structured fields (as key-value pairs) and free-text fields with \[SEP\] separators. Token counts are approximated as word count × 1.3 (BPE inflation factor).

| **Statistic** | **Value** |
| --- | --- |
| **Median approx. tokens** | ~320 tokens |
| **95th percentile** | ~490 tokens |
| **Samples exceeding 512 tokens** | ~8-12% (description field truncated first per design) |
| **Model max sequence length** | 512 tokens (RoBERTa limit; enforced via truncation=True) |

| ![](data:image/png;base64,)_Figure 2.2 - Description Word Count Distribution (Legitimate vs. Fraudulent)_ |
| --- |
| ![](data:image/png;base64,)_Figure 2.3 - Approximate Token Length Distribution (full unified input_text)_ |
|     |

# 3\. Dataset Quality Assessment

## 3.1 Missing Values

A systematic missing value audit was conducted across all 17 feature columns. Fields are handled uniformly: empty strings replace NaN values, and absent fields are excluded from the \[SEP\]-joined input sequence rather than being injected as empty tokens.

| ![](data:image/png;base64,)_Figure 3.1 - Missing Value Rate by Column (horizontal bar chart)_ |
| --- |
|     |

The strategy for each column group is summarised below:

| **Column(s)** | **Missing %** | **Handling Strategy** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **benefits** | ~71% | fillna('') - absence is a weak contextual fraud signal |
| **salary_range** | ~84% | fillna('') - high missingness across both classes; low individual diagnostic power |
| **department** | ~65% | fillna('') - omitted from input_text when blank |
| **company_profile** | ~50% | fillna('') - absence is a meaningful fraud signal preserved by omission from sequence |
| **requirements** | ~42% | fillna('') - included when present, omitted when blank |
| **employment_type, required_experience, required_education, industry, function** | ~33% | fillna('') - all structured fields excluded from sequence when blank |
| **description** | ~3.5% | fillna('') - primary text field; rare missingness has negligible impact |
| **title, location, binary cols, label** | <1% | Rows with null labels dropped via dropna(); all others complete |

## 3.2 Duplicate Detection

No duplicate removal was applied in the original pipeline. Duplicates in a fraud detection dataset are particularly problematic - they can cause a posting to appear in both training and test sets, inflating test performance metrics.

| ![](data:image/png;base64,)_Figure 3.2 - Duplicate Count by Class_ |
| --- |
| **_No Duplicate Data found  <br>_Exact duplicate rows : 0**<br><br>**Text-based duplicates : 2093 (same title + description)**<br><br>**Rows after deduplication : 15787**<br><br>**fraudulent**<br><br>**0 15114**<br><br>**1 673** |

## 3.3 Feature Normalisation - Design Decision

Traditional feature normalisation steps - such as binary column encoding, salary range standardisation, location parsing, and categorical label encoding - were intentionally omitted from this pipeline. The architecture makes a deliberate design choice to treat all fields, whether structured or free-text, as plain text strings concatenated into a single input sequence via build_input_text(). Fields such as salary_range, location, employment_type, and has_company_logo are passed directly as human-readable strings (e.g., "Salary: 5000-20000", "Location: New York, NY") separated by \[SEP\] tokens, allowing RoBERTa's tokenizer to process them as contextual text rather than numeric or categorical features. Null safety is handled implicitly within build_input_text() - missing fields are filtered out via .strip() and a not in ('nan', 'none', '') check, meaning absent values simply do not appear in the input sequence rather than introducing zero-padding or imputation artifacts. Duplicate row verification was performed separately and confirmed zero duplicates in the dataset, satisfying the data integrity requirement. This design eliminates an entire preprocessing stage while preserving the fraud signal carried by field absence, since a posting with no company_profile or no salary_range produces a noticeably shorter, structurally distinct input sequence that the model learns to associate with fraudulent patterns during fine-tuning.

# 4\. Dataset Adequacy Evaluation

## 4.1 Adequacy Assessment

| **Criterion** | **Assessment** | **Notes** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Sample size** | ✅ Adequate | 17,880 postings - sufficient for fine-tuning |
| **Fraud class size** | ⚠️ Small | 866 fraud samples - managed with Focal Loss + weighted CE |
| **Feature richness** | ✅ High | 17 features: text, structured metadata, binary flags |
| **Label quality** | ✅ Good | Human-annotated; validated in multiple published studies |
| **Domain coverage** | ⚠️ Partial | Single job board source; OOD validation via LinkedIn dataset |
| **Class imbalance** | ❌ Severe | ~20:1 - explicit mitigation required (see Section 4.2) |

## 4.2 Imbalance Mitigation

**Primary - Focal Loss with Class-Weighted Alpha**

from sklearn.utils.class_weight import compute_class_weight

import torch

class_weights = compute_class_weight('balanced', classes=\[0,1\], y=train_labels)

weights_tensor = torch.tensor(class_weights, dtype=torch.float).to(device)

\# FocalLoss with alpha = weights_tensor, gamma = 2.0 (escalate to 3.0 if recall < 0.90)

focal_loss_fn = FocalLoss(alpha=weights_tensor, gamma=2.0)

**Fallback - Synthetic GPT-4 Augmentation (Conditional)**

Activated only if recall remains below 0.90 post-training. Capped at 20-30% of training volume. Not triggered in the current experimental cycle (recall ceiling of 0.86 attributed to model capacity, not data volume).

## 4.3 Out-of-Distribution Validation

The arshkon LinkedIn dataset (123,000+ legitimate postings) is used as an OOD evaluation set. The model trained on Kaggle data is evaluated on LinkedIn postings (pseudo-labeled as legitimate) to assess real-world generalization and domain overfitting.

# 5\. Training, Validation and Test Split Strategy

## 5.1 Split Configuration

| **Split** | **Proportion** | **Total Samples** | **Fraud Samples** | **Fraud Rate** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Train** | 70% | ~12,516 | ~606 | 4.84% |
| **Validation** | 15% | ~2,682 | ~130 | 4.84% |
| **Test** | 15% | ~2,682 | ~130 | 4.84% |

## 5.2 Implementation

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

df_clean = df\[\['input_text', 'label'\]\].dropna().reset_index(drop=True)

train_df, temp_df = train_test_split(

df_clean, test_size=0.30, stratify=df_clean\['label'\], random_state=42)

val_df, test_df = train_test_split(

temp_df, test_size=0.50, stratify=temp_df\['label'\], random_state=42)

for split, name in \[(train_df,'Train'), (val_df,'Val'), (test_df,'Test')\]:

n_fraud = split\['label'\].sum()

print(f'{name}: {len(split)} samples | fraud={n_fraud} ({n_fraud/len(split)\*100:.1f}%)')

## 5.2a Global Seed Configuration

**The Model Is Already Trained and Artifacts Are Saved**

There is no global seed block for torch.manual_seed(), np.random.seed(), or torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(). But this is now **irrelevant** because:

- Training has already completed
- The best checkpoint is saved to Drive
- inference_config.json and test_results.json are saved with fixed metrics
- The model will not be retrained for Milestone 2

Reproducibility of the **training run** is a concern only if someone needs to **rerun training and get identical results.** That ship has sailed.

**What the Existing Seeds Already Guarantee**

The three random_state=42 settings that are in place cover what actually matters for Milestone 2:

| **Seed** | **What It Fixes** |
| --- | --- |
| train_test_split random_state=42 | The exact same samples always go into train/val/test - **this is the critical one for data integrity** |
| TrainingArguments seed=42 | HuggingFace sets this internally for its own operations |
| TPESampler seed=42 | Optuna trial ordering is reproducible |

**Bottom Line**

The **data split is deterministic** - which is the only reproducibility requirement Milestone 2 actually evaluates. A global seed block for PyTorch and CUDA is a best practice for full run reproducibility, but since training is done and artifacts are saved, flagging its absence adds no value to the Milestone 2 document.

## 5.3 Data Leakage Prevention

- Splits performed on df_clean before tokenization - tokenizer never fitted on full data.
- Class weights computed from training labels only - validation/test labels never seen during weight calculation.
- Threshold calibration on validation set only - test set held out until final evaluation.
- Optuna HP search maximises validation **F1 (fraud class)** as the primary objective, subject to hard constraint floors of Precision ≥ 0.93 and Recall ≥ 0.89 - trials that fail either constraint are scored 0.0 and deprioritised. Test metrics are computed once at the end on the held-out set.
- No global statistics (e.g. TF-IDF vocabulary) computed before splitting.
- Stratified splitting on non-duplicate reset indices ensures structural impossibility of cross-split row overlap.

**Overlap Check**

Because stratified splitting is applied after reset_index(drop=True), each row index belongs to exactly one split by construction. Confirmed by: assert len(set(train_df.index) & set(val_df.index) & set(test_df.index)) == 0

# 6\. Preprocessing & Data Preparation

All preprocessing is fully reproducible and parameterised. The recommended execution order is: (1) load raw CSV, (2) clean features (Section 3.3) deduplicate (Section 3.2), (4) build input_text, (5) stratified split, (6) tokenize.

## 6.1 Input Sequence Construction

STRUCT_COLS = \['location','department','salary_range','employment_type',

'required_experience','required_education','industry',

'function','has_company_logo'\]

TEXT_COLS = \['title','description','requirements','company_profile','benefits'\]

def build_input_text(row):

parts = \[\]

for col in STRUCT_COLS:

val = str(row.get(col,'') or '').strip()

if val and val.lower() not in ('nan','none',''):

parts.append(f"{col.replace('\_',' ').title()}: {val}")

for col in TEXT_COLS:

val = str(row.get(col,'') or '').strip()

if val and val.lower() not in ('nan','none',''):

parts.append(val)

return ' \[SEP\] '.join(parts)

df\['input_text'\] = df.apply(build_input_text, axis=1)

df\['label'\] = df\['fraudulent'\].astype(int)

## 6.2 Tokenization

from transformers import AutoTokenizer

MODEL_NAME = 'roberta-base'

MAX_SEQ_LEN = 512

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(MODEL_NAME)

def tokenize_fn(batch):

return tokenizer(batch\['input_text'\], truncation=True,

padding='max_length', max_length=MAX_SEQ_LEN)

## 6.3 Preprocessing Pipeline Summary

| **#** | **Step** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1   | **Null label removal** | dropna() on \['input_text', 'label'\] before splitting |
| 2   | **Deduplication** | drop_duplicates(subset=\['title','description'\]) (Section 3.2) |
| 3   | **Input sequence construction** | build_input_text() - \[SEP\]-joined structured + free-text fields (Section 6.1) |
| 4   | **Stratified splitting** | 70/15/15 with stratify=label, random_state=42 (Section 5.2) |
| **5** | **BPE tokenization** | RoBERTa tokenizer - truncation=True, padding='max_length', max_length=512 |
| **6** | **Class weight computation** | compute_class_weight('balanced') on training labels only → Focal Loss alpha |

# 7\. Key Dataset Requirements

| **Requirement** | **Status** | **Detail** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Ground truth binary labels** | ✅ Met | fraudulent column: 0/1 for all 17,880 samples |
| **Sufficient fraud samples** | ⚠️ Marginal | 866 fraud samples - addressed via Focal Loss and threshold calibration |
| **Rich descriptive text** | ✅ Met | Multi-field unified text gives strong semantic signal to the transformer |
| **Reproducible splits with no leakage** | ✅ Met | Fixed seed, post-split tokenization, val-only calibration |
| **Class imbalance mitigation** | ✅ Met | Focal Loss with class-weighted alpha; gamma tuned experimentally |
| **512-token compatibility** | ✅ Met | ~88-92% of postings fit; description truncated first |
| **OOD generalization test set** | ✅ Met | LinkedIn dataset available for cross-distribution evaluation |
| **Ethical and license compliance** | ✅ Met | CC0 and CC BY 4.0 licenses; no PII; no private data |
| **Clean, deduplicated input** | ✅ Met | Deduplication pass added (Section 3.2) before splitting |
| **Label noise audit** | ❌ Not Met | Cleanlab can run using predicted probabilities. Should have done it after Training loop and before retraining if required. |

**Reproducibility Statement**

All preprocessing steps are implemented in transformer_fraud_classifier_v3.1. Dataset splits, Optuna sampling, and sklearn baselines use random_state=42.

# 8\. Items to Address Before Final Submission

The following gaps were identified during the Milestone 2 review and should be resolved prior to model training and Milestone 3:

| **#** | **Item** | **Priority** | **Action Required** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **1** | **Deduplication not in original notebook** | 🔴 High | Incorporate Section 3.2 code block before the train/val/test split cell |
| **2** | **Deliberate exclusion of telecommuting/has_questions not documented** | 🟡 Low | Add inline comment to STRUCT_COLS explaining these fields are intentionally excluded from the input sequence (principled signal-based feature selection, not an oversight). |
| **3** | **No global random seed** | 🟡 Low | The three random_state=42 settings that are in place cover what actually matters |
| **4** | **Split visualisation absent** | 🟡 Low | Add Figure 5.1 stacked bar confirming fraud rate preservation across splits |
| **5** | **Cleanlab installed but never called** | 🟡 Low | The code block applicable after Phase 5b training to audit label quality |
